finding_results.notes: Revised four tail-end responses in the canal exchange where Anya had collapsed into neutral monosyllables. Line 251: 'Yes' expanded to 'Yes. It was just watching. I wasn't going in.' — tangential honesty deflecting from the actual question (alone?) to a different truth (just surveillance). Line 257: 'I'm fine, Mama' replaced with 'I know the route. I've got layers.' — answers the safety concern underneath the coat question rather than the surface question, revealing operational familiarity with the area. Line 261: 'Okay' replaced with 'I'll come by. Tomorrow, maybe.' — Anya's natural directness producing commitment instead of noncommittal filler. Line 265: 'I'll try it' expanded to 'It's probably not that bad. I'll try it.' — over-quick reassurance about the salt worry before Yor has fully landed her apology, consistent with Anya's pattern of responding faster than the conversation warrants. These join the already-strong earlier lines (line 221 self-correction, line 229 excess operational knowledge, line 233 concealment-pressure directive) to produce an exchange where Anya is identifiable from speech alone throughout. No line in the revised exchange reads as interchangeable generic filial dialogue.

summary: Single finding addressed. Four generic monosyllabic Anya responses in the canal scene's domestic tail were replaced with lines carrying character-specific concealment pressure: tangential honesty, operational-knowledge slips, blunt commitment, and over-quick reassurance. The stronger Anya-specific dialogue earlier in the exchange (self-correction at line 221, excess knowledge at line 229, the 'don't make whatever call comes next' directive at line 233) was preserved unchanged. Scene restraint and cover integrity maintained — no confessional material added.
